title: fuchsia rivularis subsp. pubescens
intro: Fuchsia rivularis subsp. pubescens is a subspecies of flowering plant in the fuchsia family, native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Fuchsia rivularis subsp. pubescens include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as wound healing and skin infections. Fuchsia rivularis subsp. pubescens is prepared as a decoction or infusion in herbal medicine, often used as a topical application or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
